refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall provide worksite area analysis including terrain representation.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR combined multiple obligations: terrain, obstacles, and work-zone representation. To ensure atomicity and clarity, it is necessary to separate these into distinct sub-FRs, each focusing on a single aspect of worksite area analysis. This refinement isolates terrain representation as one atomic FR.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify that the system can generate and provide a detailed and accurate representation of the terrain within the worksite area, independent of obstacle or work-zone data.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall provide worksite area analysis including terrain representation.
The NGHE solution shall provide worksite area analysis including obstacle representation.
The NGHE solution shall provide worksite area analysis including work-zone representation.

candidate_dps: Generate a digital terrain model representing the physical features of the worksite
Create a semantic map annotating key site features relevant to planning and safety
Develop a unified worksite model integrating terrain and operational policy constraints
Produce separate data layers for terrain and site features accessible to planning and execution modules
selected_dp.selected_dp: Produce separate data layers for terrain and site features accessible to planning and execution modules

potential_sources: Integration of terrain and site feature data layers
Interfaces between data layers and planning/execution functions
coupling_check.notes: By separating terrain representation and site feature annotations into distinct data layers, the design supports modular updates and reduces coupling. Planning and execution functions can access only the relevant layers, promoting independence.

assumptions_risks: Assumes that terrain and site features can be effectively represented as separate data layers without loss of necessary context
Risk that inconsistent updates between layers could lead to planning errors
Assumes planning and execution functions can integrate multiple data layers seamlessly
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Clarify the scope and format of the worksite model/map to explicitly define data layer boundaries and interfaces to ensure modularity and reduce integration risks.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Contains data management and processing for terrain and site features, providing data layers accessible to planning and execution modules

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Exchange of sensor and status data relevant to terrain and site features

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives commands and requests from planning and execution modules for data access and updates

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as data layers are core to NGHE processing; low diffusion since data is centralized here.
Vehicle module is focused on physical control, poor containment of data layers, would cause high diffusion.
Teleoperation is for remote control, not data management; poor containment and high diffusion.
Safety Services unrelated to terrain/site data layers; very poor containment and high diffusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary to contain the DP because it logically owns the data layers for terrain and site features, which are essential inputs for planning and execution modules. This centralization minimizes diffusion and maintains cohesion.
containment_risks: Potential coupling with planning and execution modules if interfaces are not well defined.
Risk of NGHE Solution becoming a bottleneck if data access is not efficiently managed.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP clearly addresses the FR by specifying the production of separate data layers for terrain and site features, which directly supports the requirement for worksite area analysis including terrain representation.

diffusion_risks: Potential coupling with planning and execution modules if interfaces are not well defined.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module has high containment (score 8) and low diffusion (score 2), indicating good architectural packaging of the DP. Other modules show poor containment and high diffusion, supporting the choice of NGHE Solution as the IM boundary.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible by confirming that the NGHE Solution module provides the required data layers accessible to planning and execution modules, as per acceptance criteria.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The ownership candidate NGHE Solution module clearly owns the implementation of the DP, with well-defined interfaces for telemetry and tasking, ensuring clear allocation of responsibilities.

recommended_revisions: Define and document clear interface contracts between NGHE Solution and planning/execution modules to minimize coupling risks.
Implement efficient data access mechanisms within NGHE Solution to prevent bottlenecks during data retrieval and updates.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed with clear justification and acceptable architectural packaging. The NGHE Solution module is identified as the implementation module with high containment and low diffusion, minimizing architectural risks. Verification feasibility and allocation clarity are confirmed. Although there are coupling risks, these are addressed by recommended revisions rather than requiring further decomposition. Given the high safety criticality, it is prudent to finalize the current decomposition level and focus on implementing the recommended interface contracts and data access mechanisms to mitigate risks.

revise.why: To define and document clear interface contracts between NGHE Solution and planning/execution modules to minimize coupling risks, and to implement efficient data access mechanisms within NGHE Solution to prevent bottlenecks.

assumptions: The NGHE Solution module can encapsulate the DP effectively with well-defined interfaces.
Verification methods are sufficient to confirm the implementation meets the FR.
Recommended revisions will be implemented to mitigate coupling and performance risks.
open_risks: Potential coupling with planning and execution modules if interfaces are not well defined.
evidence_needed: Documentation of interface contracts between NGHE Solution and other modules.
Performance metrics demonstrating efficient data access within NGHE Solution module.
Verification results confirming data layers meet acceptance criteria.
